front: Building: bullet-shaped Barcelona skyscraper originally built for a water company, designed to evoke an erupting geyser or waterspout
Building: Barcelona tower inspired by the nearby Montserrat mountain, with 4,400–4,500 pixelated square LED-covered windows
Building: Nouvel described this Barcelona tower's shape as phallic; its floors take an oval/elliptical plan
Building: Barcelona skyscraper whose temperature sensors regulate motorized external blinds
Building: Paris institute whose south facade has 240 motor-controlled 'iris' apertures inspired by Islamic mashrabiya screens
back: Arab World Institute (Institut du Monde Arabe), Paris (Jean Nouvel)
work: Arab World Institute (Institut du Monde Arabe), Paris

front: Building: Paris building with a high-tech metallic brise soleil whose motorized irises open and close like camera shutters to regulate light
back: Arab World Institute (Institut du Monde Arabe), Paris (Jean Nouvel)
work: Arab World Institute (Institut du Monde Arabe), Paris

front: Building: museum on Saadiyat Island, UAE, with an eight-layered floating dome whose web-like pattern creates a 'rain of light' effect as if filtered through date palm fronds
Building: UAE museum described as the first universal art museum to open in the 21st century, whose dome has 8,000 medallions
Building: Abu Dhabi museum where Leonardo's Salvator Mundi was briefly slated for display
Building: Minneapolis theater featuring a cantilevered lobby called the 'Endless Bridge' jutting toward the Mississippi River
Building: Paris museum designed with Patrick Blanc's exterior 'green wall' of living plants, built without barriers so visitors could move freely between world art exhibits
back: Musée du quai Branly, Paris (Jean Nouvel)

front: Building: illuminated blue cube concert hall for the Danish Broadcasting Corporation, described as the most expensive concert hall ever built
Building: Copenhagen concert hall whose interior drew inspiration from Hans Scharoun's 'vineyard' design for Berlin's Philharmonie
Building: historic French opera house Nouvel redesigned by adding a semi-cylindrical glass and steel double vault; the building is named after him
Architect: French architect who won the 2008 Pritzker Prize
Architect: described his design method as finding 'singularities' in objects, a concept developed in a book co-written with a philosopher
Architect: placed a massive rusty cube called 'Monolith' on a Swiss lake for the 2002 Swiss Expo
Architect: designed the National Museum of Qatar in Doha, with interlocking discs inspired by the desert rose crystal
Architect: designed part of the Leeum Samsung Museum of Art in Seoul alongside Mario Botta and Rem Koolhaas
Architect: turned down the Dancing House in Prague, which was subsequently designed by Frank Gehry and Vlado Milunic
Architect: advocated for traditional hand tools in the reconstruction of Notre-Dame de Paris
Architect: designed a building in Nicosia with balconies of various heights suggesting the structure is 'breathing'
